blockingIssues.description: The rerun removes the unsafe raw-byte hashing and keeps the public `InputItem` API unchanged, but the sidecar marker scheme can collide with legitimate user JSON objects shaped like `{"__agents_internal_empty_object_identity_<n>__": "__agents_internal_empty_object_identity_<n>__"}` and silently strip them to `{}` during `session_input_callback` attribution.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax the `runtime-parity-worker` mandatory red/green requirement for safety-only reruns where the bug is internal implementation safety rather than user-visible semantics, so workers can isolate helper-only regressions without inventing an externally observable failing test first.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `fix-session-provenance-safe-empty-object-sidecar-identity` cites the worker handoff and transcript skeleton showing a recorded deviation from `.factory/skills/runtime-parity-worker/SKILL.md` because the issue was an unsafe internal identity mechanism rather than a missing observable semantic.
